what_hr_looks_for: Relevant experience and skills matching the JD
Keywords matching the ATS (Applicant Tracking System)
resume_tips: Use action verbs: Built, Designed, Led, Reduced, Improved, Launched
Quantify impact: 'Improved API response time by 35%', not 'worked on API improvements'
Tailor resume keywords to match the specific job description
Keep to 1 page for <5 years experience, max 2 pages for senior roles
Use a clean ATS-friendly format — avoid tables, columns, graphics

what_to_expect: Why are you interested in this role?
preparation_tips: Have your resume in front of you
Research the company's recent news and products
Have 2-3 thoughtful questions ready for the recruiter
Be in a quiet location with good phone signal

common_formats: Live coding on shared IDE (HackerRank, CoderPad)
preparation_tips: Think aloud — interviewers evaluate your reasoning process
Clarify requirements before coding (edge cases, constraints)
Start with brute force, then optimize
Write clean, readable code with meaningful variable names
Test your code with example inputs before declaring done
Prepare 8-10 STAR stories covering different competencies
Each story should be under 3 minutes when spoken
Have stories that can answer multiple question themes
Be honest — authenticity matters more than polish
End every story with a lesson learned or long-term impact
Prepare thoughtful questions about team culture, challenges, roadmap
Be ready to discuss your 30-60-90 day plan
Show genuine enthusiasm and specific knowledge about the company

common_hr_questions.question: Tell me about yourself.
common_hr_questions.guidance: Start with current role/status (30s), highlight 2 relevant past experiences (45s), explain future goals and why this role (30s). Total: ~2 minutes.

common_hr_questions.question: What is your greatest strength?
common_hr_questions.guidance: State the strength, give a specific work example, explain the impact. Align with the role requirements.

common_hr_questions.question: What is your greatest weakness?
common_hr_questions.guidance: Give a real (but not disqualifying) weakness. Show self-awareness. Describe concrete steps you are taking to improve. Show progress.

common_hr_questions.question: Why do you want to work here?
common_hr_questions.guidance: Research thoroughly. Connect 3 things: company mission/product you admire, specific role details that excite you, how it fits your career growth.

common_hr_questions.question: Where do you see yourself in 5 years?
common_hr_questions.guidance: Show growth ambition, contribution to company goals, and implicit commitment to stay and grow with the company.

common_hr_questions.question: Why are you leaving your current job?
common_hr_questions.guidance: Never badmouth. Focus on growth opportunities you seek. Frame as moving toward something, not away from something.

common_hr_questions.question: Do you have any questions for us?
common_hr_questions.guidance: Always have 3-4 questions ready. Ask about: team culture, biggest challenge in the role, how success is measured, growth opportunities.

virtual_interview: Test camera, microphone, and internet 30 minutes before
Use a clean, professional background or virtual background
Look at the camera (not the screen) to simulate eye contact
Use structured pauses — silence is better than filler words
Dress professionally from head to toe (builds confidence)
Have a glass of water nearby
in_person_interview: Bring printed copies of your resume
Take brief notes if allowed — shows engagement

content: Reference a specific topic from the conversation
Reiterate your enthusiasm for the role
follow_up.example: Dear [Name], Thank you for taking the time to speak with me today about the [Role] position at [Company]. Our discussion about [specific technical topic or project] reinforced my excitement about contributing to your team. I look forward to hearing about the next steps. Best regards, [Your Name]

principles: Always negotiate — most offers have 10-20% flexibility
Have a competing offer or market data to anchor negotiation
Negotiate total compensation: base, bonus, equity, signing bonus, benefits
Be collaborative, not adversarial — 'I'm very excited about this offer...'
Get the offer in writing before making decisions

Amazon.preparation_tip: Have 1-2 STAR stories for each LP. Amazon interviewers explicitly map stories to LPs.

Google.preparation_tip: Google focuses heavily on how you think. Show your reasoning process, not just the answer.

IBM.preparation_tip: IBM interviewers look for alignment with IBM values. Research IBM's current focus areas (AI, Cloud, Sustainability).

Microsoft.preparation_tip: Microsoft shifted from 'know-it-alls' to 'learn-it-alls'. Emphasize learning from failure and growth.
